english: means of the side); gló-ča (glo cha) (Ld. *ldó-ča* (ldo cha)) ornaments, suspended on the side of the body, strings of pearls, shells etc., worn by women in the girdle; also in a general sense: sráṅ-gí glo yýas yyón-na (srang gi glo g.yas g.yon na) on both sides of the street Stg.; perh. also side of a house, wall, in the expression: *kún-me lo tol* (kun me lo tol) the thieves broke through the wall W.; glo-skár (glo skar) window Pth. -- 2. saddle-girth W. -- 3. cough, *lo gyág-pa* (glo rgyag pa) C. to cough; (Sch. has: to err, to act foolishly, to lose, to neglect); *lo láṅ-wa* (glo lang ba) C. to cough; gló-ku sra (glo khu sra) a bad cough Sch.; *lo-kóg* (glo kog) C., W., cough; glo-rgyál (glo rgyal) Lt. a chronic cough; glo-bstúd (glo bstud) Lt. a permanent short cough. -- 4. Not quite clear is the etymology in glo rdég-pa (glo rdeg pa) Sch.: to be frightened, timid, and glo rdég (-tu) (glo rdeg tu) suddenly, = gló-bur (glo bur) q.v.

the lungs, gló-ba ma lṅa (glo ba ma lnga) prob. the five posterior lobes of the lungs, gló-ba bu lṅa (glo ba bu lnga) the five anterior ones Med.; gló-k'a (glo kha) of a colour like the blood of the lungs, pale-red Sch.; glo-dóṅ (glo dong) windpipe Cs. -- glo-rdól (glo rdol) a disease of the lungs; glo-rkó (glo rko) perh. the same. -- glo-sbúbs (glo sbubs) (Sch. spub (spub)) wind-pipe. -- *gló-ro* (glo ro) W. prob. pulmonary consumption. -- glo lú-ba (glo lu ba) Lt. 'convulsion of the lungs' Cs., or simply: cough, v. lú-ba (lu ba).

1. suddenly, instantaneously, also gló-bur-du (glo bur du), glo-bur-bar (glo bur bar); glo-bur-du mi maṅ-po °čhi-bai sdug-bsṅál (glo bur du mi mang po 'chi ba'i sdug bsngal) the calamity of many men dying suddenly; glo-bur-nád (glo bur nad) diseases that arise on a sudden (opp. to lhan-skyés (lhan skyes) inherited diseases) Med. -- gló-bur-ba (glo bur ba) adj., gló-bur-bai don (glo bur ba'i don) the signification of suddenness Lex. -- 2. Cs.: 'recently, gló-bur-du óṅs-pa (glo bur du 'ongs pa) a new comer'.
a rise, an elevation above a surface Sch.

(Bal. and Kh. *ylog* (glog)), col. also glóg ka (glog ka), lightning, flash of lightning; glog bar (glog 'bar) it lightens; glog kyug (glog 'khyug) id.; glog kyug-pai yun tsám-las ma lón-par (glog 'khyug pa'i yun tsam las ma lon par) with the rapidity of lightning Mil.; glog rgyú-ba (glog rgyu ba) the flashing of light, Dzl.; glog-sprín (glog sprin) thunder-cloud, also as a symbol of the transitoriness of things.

1. to loosen, relax, slacken vb. a. Cs. -- 2. to comfort, console; to cheer up Sch.; glód-la rgyún-du bźugs (glod la rgyun du bzhugs) your honour may be easy about staying here always Mil., cf. lhód-pa (lhod pa). -- 3. Ü: to give, ma bzuṅ ma glód(-par) (ma bzung ma glod par) without any regard to taking or giving Glr.
1. to return an answer, to reply. -- 2. to patch, to mend, cf. klón-pa (klon pa) etc.

n. of a mountain in Lh., perh. incor. instead of gaṇ-dho-la (gaN dho la) q.v.; it may also be derived from घण्टा (ghaN+TA) bell, and thus the word would signify the same as dril-bu-ri (dril bu ri), which is the name of another holy mountain, at the foot of which the nobleman's seat Gondla is situated.

(Ld. col. *yá-če* (ya ce)) I. vb. to rejoice, to be rejoiced or glad, la at, in, or of; dé-la dgá-ste (de la dga' ste), rejoiced at it, glad of it, -- mi dgá-ste (mi dga' ste) grieved, vexed, indignant at it; khrims yód-pa-la dgá-nas (khrims yod pa la dga' nas) if you wish to have the law introduced Glr.; gsód-pa-la dgá-źiṅ (gsod pa la dga' zhing) sanguinary, delighting in blood-shed Dzl.; bu-mo de-nyíd-la dgá-bas (bu mo de nyid la dga' bas), as I wish to have none other but this girl Dzl.; bód-la dgá-ba yčig kyaṅ ma byuṅ (bod la dga' ba gcig kyang ma byung) nobody took an interest in Tibet Glr.; khyed čii phyir mi dga (khyed ci'i phyir mi dga') why are you so dejected, low-spirited? dga bźin-du (dga' bzhin du) with pleasure (e.g. I shall accept it); rarely with the gerund: bram-ze da-ruṅ °dug-ste rab-tu dga-nas (bram ze da rung 'dug ste rab tu dga' nas) much rejoicing, very glad, when (that) the Brahmin was still there Dzl.; with the termin. of the inf.: to do a thing readily, willingly, nyán-par dgá-ba (nyan par dga' ba) to like
